description_long: This is a modern, military-style helicopter with a sleek, aerodynamic fuselage painted in a matte dark gray or black finish. It features a large main rotor with five long, straight blades and a smaller tail rotor with five blades, both constructed from what appears to be composite materials. The cockpit is equipped with multiple angular, tinted windows that wrap around the front and sides, providing wide visibility for the pilots. The helicopter has robust, angular landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel, rather than traditional skids, suggesting it is designed for both urban and rugged environments. Subtle markings and alphanumeric codes are visible on the fuselage, adding to its realistic detailing. The overall design is compact yet powerful, suitable for rapid deployment and maneuverability in urban scenarios.
description: A sleek, dark gray military helicopter with a five-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and wheeled landing gear, detailed with visible markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, five-blade main rotor, tail rotor, and detailed landing gear.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the angular cockpit windows, nose profile, and symmetrical rotor blade arrangement.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the main and tail rotors, landing gear, and visible fuselage markings.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail rotor, tail structure, and the alignment of the landing gear.
attribute_rotor_blades: Five long, straight main rotor blades, likely composite material, matte black with no visible markings, each blade approximately 6-7 meters in length.
attribute_tail_rotor: Five-blade tail rotor, mounted vertically at the end of the tail boom, matte black, no visible markings, partially enclosed by a protective structure.
attribute_landing_skids: No traditional skids; instead, the helicopter features robust, angular wheeled landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel, constructed from metal and rubber.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four large, angular cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, tinted light blue, with visible black framing and no additional markings.
attribute_registration_number: Visible on the side: 'N15' and 'SVJX', white alphanumeric markings.
